text:
Once upon a snowy afternoon, in a small quaint village surrounded by mountains, there lived a wise old owl named Rufus. Rufus was no ordinary owl. He knew a lot about history and stories. Every December, he liked to share one of his own.

So one snowy day, Rufus decided to tell the kids about the Moon Whisperers. It was said that long ago, people could hear whispers from the moon. They'd listen at midnight.

Rufus began his tale. "Once upon an age, folks out in the fields would hear lovely tunes coming from the moon. They thought it was the spirits of the crops."

He shivered. "I've felt their cool warmth myself."

The children listened. They imagined tiny silver rabbits singing lullabies.

"But why did they stop?" asked a bold little goose named Geela.

Rufus scratched his chin. "Maybe because humans started keeping clocks. Makes you sleepy listening to music."

They laughed. Later, they decided
